# Contributing to Samaris OS

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Samaris OS! We welcome contributions from the community to help build and improve this operating system.

## Table of Contents

- [Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)
- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
- [How to Contribute](#how-to-contribute)
  - [Reporting Bugs](#reporting-bugs)
  - [Suggesting Features](#suggesting-features)
  - [Improving Documentation](#improving-documentation)
  - [Submitting Code Changes](#submitting-code-changes)
- [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
- [Style Guidelines](#style-guidelines)
- [Communication](#communication)

## Code of Conduct

All contributors are expected to maintain a respectful and constructive environment. Harassment, discrimination, or any form of toxic behavior will not be tolerated.

## Getting Started

1. Fork the repository.
2. Clone your fork locally.
3. Set up the build environment as described in the project documentation.
4. Create a new branch for your work: `git checkout -b my-feature-branch`.

## How to Contribute

### Reporting Bugs

Use the **Bug Report** issue template. Include as much detail as possible: OS version, architecture, build environment, reproduction steps, and relevant logs.

### Suggesting Features

Use the **Feature Request** issue template. Clearly describe the problem, your proposed solution, and the impact on Samaris OS.

### Improving Documentation

Use the **Documentation** issue template or submit a pull request directly with documentation improvements.

### Submitting Code Changes

1. Ensure your code follows the project's style and conventions.
2. Write clear, concise commit messages.
3. Keep pull requests focused on a single concern.
4. Link your PR to any relevant issues.
5. Ensure the project builds and tests pass before submitting.

## Development Workflow

- All changes should go through pull requests and be reviewed before merging.
- The `main` branch is kept stable. Feature work should happen on separate branches.
- Squash commits if appropriate to keep history clean.

## Style Guidelines

- Follow the existing code style and conventions used throughout the project.
- Write meaningful comments for non-obvious logic.
- Keep functions and modules focused on a single responsibility.

## Communication

- **Maintainer:** Khaled Ben Taieb
- **Email:** contact.samaris.os@gmail.com
- **Website:** [samaris.tech](https://samaris.tech)

For questions, discussions, or support, open a GitHub Discussion or reach out via email.

Thank you for helping make Samaris OS better!
